title: Incorrect type returned in Python code node
symptom: The Python code node returns <class ‘pyodide.ffi.JsProxy’> instead of the expected type for input JSON values.
applies_when: Input JSON contains values wrapped in JsProxy
not_this_if: Input JSON values are not wrapped in JsProxy
root_cause: The Python code node is returning a JsProxy object instead of the actual Python type. This occurs because the input values are being wrapped in a proxy object that does not directly expose their underlying types.
fix_steps: Modify the code to use the 'to_py()' method on the JsProxy object.
Return the actual type and instance check using the modified code.
agent_instruction: Use the 'to_py()' method to get the actual type of the input value in the Python code node.
